name: Ascent to El Palmeral via the Vereda de Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena — circular from Don Benito
name_original: Subida a El Palmeral por la Vereda de Don Venito a Vilanueva de la Serena — circular desde Don Benito
region_intro: Hiking around Don Benito offers a variety of routes through the Extremaduran landscape, characterized by river valleys and agricultural areas. The region features pathways along the Guadiana River and the Canal del Zújar, providing accessible terrain. Further afield, areas like Santa Natalia present natural features such as a waterfall during rainy seasons and a bridge over the Guadámez River. La Serrezuela, to the south, is known for its holm oak meadows and granite outcrops. Best hiking trails around Don Benito The most popular hiking route is Don Benito by the Canal del Zújar — circular, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This easy route offers accessible walks along the Canal del Zújar. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to El Palmeral via the Vereda de Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena — circular from Don Benito, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This route follows an old railway line, providing an accessible path between towns. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Urbanización La Zarza, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Don Benito is defined by riverside paths, holm oak meadows, and granite outcrops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate hikes. The routes in Don Benito are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 140 hikers have used komoot to explore Don Benito's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Don Benito?
A: There are over 19 hiking trails around Don Benito, offering a variety of experiences. The majority, 13 routes, are rated as easy, while 6 are moderate, making the area accessible for different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Don Benito?
A: Don Benito experiences mild winters and hot summers. The most pleasant times for hiking are autumn and spring, when temperatures are moderate and rainfall is more likely, especially for experiencing natural features like the Santa Natalia waterfall. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are advisable if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Don Benito?
A: Yes, Don Benito offers several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293545291/don-benito-by-the-canal-del-zujar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito by the Canal del Zújar — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 km long and takes about 1 hour 40 minutes. This route provides accessible walks along the canal, perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Don Benito area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Don Benito are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293551962/ascent-to-el-palmeral-via-the-vereda-de-don-venito-to-vilanueva-de-la-serena-circular-from-don-benito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Palmeral via the Vereda de Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena — circular from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8 km loop that follows an old railway line, offering a scenic and accessible path.

Q: Are there any hiking trails near Don Benito that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, during the rainy season, the natural area of Santa Natalia, located about 15 kilometers from Don Benito, features a notable waterfall. This area is part of the Natura 2000 network and offers rich natural scenery, including a bridge over the Guadámez River.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Don Benito?
A: Hiking around Don Benito offers diverse landscapes. You'll find riverside paths along the Guadiana River and Canal del Zújar, holm oak meadows, Mediterranean scrubland, and picturesque granite outcrops, particularly in areas like La Serrezuela. The broader Extremaduran landscape also features river valleys and agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic stops.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Don Benito?
A: The trails in Don Benito are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 16 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible paths, the natural beauty of the riverside routes, and the varied terrain that offers something for everyone.

Q: Are there any trails that follow old railway lines in Don Benito?
A: Yes, the Vereda de Don Benito a Villanueva de la Serena is a natural path that utilizes an old railway line. This accessible and easy route connects Don Benito with Villanueva de la Serena, offering a flat path ideal for nature lovers. A specific route that incorporates this is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293551962/ascent-to-el-palmeral-via-the-vereda-de-don-venito-to-vilanueva-de-la-serena-circular-from-don-benito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Palmeral via the Vereda de Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena — circular from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes around Don Benito?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, some offer a longer distance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19409747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Urbanización La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.4 km trail that typically takes around 3 hours 27 minutes to complete, providing a more extended experience through varied terrain.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while hiking in Don Benito?
A: Yes, the region's natural areas, particularly those along the Guadiana River and within the Natura 2000 network, are ecologically important and can be excellent for birdwatching. There is even a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; listed as a highlight near the trails.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs near Don Benito that can be explored during a hike?
A: Yes, the area features several water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are notable, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These areas can offer scenic views and opportunities for aquatic sports or fishing, and some hiking routes may pass nearby.
tips:
Don Benito is a city that surprises both visitors and residents. The historic center, with its Plaza de España, the Church of Santiago Apóstol, and Renaissance buildings, attracts visitors.
Outstanding museums such as the Ethnographic Museum (rated 4.6/5) and the Automobile Museum are well-received.
Urban parks like Tierno Galván, the Palmeral, and Las Albercas offer green spaces appreciated by tourists and locals alike.

“El Palmeral”: The Don Benito City Council has created this hiking route so that any person or family can enjoy a tour of this natural environment. The Palmeral is surrounded by palm trees and has a small hill where a structure reminiscent of the Greek Parthenon has been built.
If you have the opportunity, I recommend you visit this beautiful place. 😊
Beautiful road that connects both towns and is constantly surrounded by agricultural fields with panoramic views of the area. La Vereda is one of those old paths that have been used for many years!

waypoints.name: Vereda from Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena
